description: Introduction to Natural Language Processing is a comprehensive course that delves into the fundamental concepts and techniques of natural language processing, including text preprocessing, tokenization, sentiment analysis, named entity recognition, and machine translation. This course will explore the theoretical foundations of NLP, as well as practical applications and state-of-the-art techniques, with a focus on deep learning models and their implementation using popular libraries such as NLTK, spaCy, and TensorFlow.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of data structures and algorithms
Familiarity with linear algebra and calculus
Introduction to machine learning concepts and deep learning frameworks
